core_mechanism: formation and splitting of self-localized solitary waves (solitons) in a driven-dissipative nonlinear Schrödinger / Gross-Pitaevskii field, where dispersion balances a self-interaction nonlinearity and the number of stable localized structures bifurcates as a saturable gain reservoir coupled to the field is spatially retuned
brief_reason: The abstract models soliton formation with Gross-Pitaevskii equations for a coupled two-component field driven by a gain/reservoir, and shows the transition between single and multiple solitons is controlled by the position of that gain reservoir.
